core_concept: Art Styles: Elements, Landscape, Abstraction, Sculpture, Architecture
facts: Landscape art shows outdoor scenes like mountains, trees, or rivers, not indoor rooms or portraits.
Abstract art uses shapes and colors to express feelings or ideas without trying to look like a real object, so a painting of a red circle might represent anger, not an apple.
A sculpture is a three-dimensional artwork that you can walk around and see from all sides, unlike a drawing which is flat.
Architecture is the art of designing buildings that are both useful (like a house to live in) and beautiful (like a fancy church), so a school building needs to be safe and also pleasant to look at.
misconceptions: Abstract art has no meaning because it doesn't look like a real thing.
A sculpture is the same as a drawing because both are art and can be hung on a wall.
